annotations.label: Graph coloring

anchor.snippet: graph coloring is a methodic assignment of labels

annotations.note: `SimpleGraph.Coloring α` is a proper coloring defined as a hom into the complete graph on the color type.

annotations.label: Five color theorem

anchor.snippet: every planar map can be colored with no more than five colors

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of planar graphs/maps, so the five color theorem is absent.

annotations.label: Proper vertex coloring

anchor.snippet: namely a labeling of the graph's vertices with colors

annotations.note: `SimpleGraph.Coloring` is exactly a proper vertex coloring (adjacent vertices get different colors via `Coloring.valid`).

annotations.label: k-coloring

anchor.snippet: is called a (proper) k -coloring

annotations.note: `SimpleGraph.Colorable n` (nonempty `Coloring (Fin n)`) is the proposition that G has a proper n-coloring.

annotations.label: Chromatic number

anchor.snippet: is called its chromatic number

annotations.note: `SimpleGraph.chromaticNumber` is the minimal number of colors (an `ℕ∞`), matching the article's definition.

annotations.label: k-colorable and k-chromatic

anchor.snippet: is k -colorable , and it is k -chromatic if its chromatic number is exactly k

annotations.note: k-colorable is `Colorable k` and being exactly k-chromatic is captured by `chromaticNumber_eq_iff_colorable_not_colorable`.

annotations.label: Color class

anchor.snippet: is called a color class

annotations.note: `Coloring.colorClass c` is the set of vertices colored `c`, and `colorClasses` collects them.

annotations.label: k-coloring as partition into independent sets

anchor.snippet: a k -coloring is the same as a partition of the vertex set into k independent sets

annotations.note: `partitionable_iff_colorable` proves n-partitionability into independent sets is equivalent to n-colorability.

annotations.label: Three-coloring count example

anchor.snippet: the graph in the adjacent image can be colored in 12 ways

annotations.note: This specific counting example depends on chromatic polynomials, which are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chromatic polynomial

anchor.snippet: that counts the number of t -colorings of G

annotations.note: No chromatic polynomial is defined in Mathlib (it is listed as a TODO in the coloring file).

annotations.label: Chromatic number from chromatic polynomial

anchor.snippet: χ is the smallest positive integer that is not a zero of the chromatic polynomial

annotations.note: Requires the chromatic polynomial, which is not formalized.

annotations.label: Edge coloring

anchor.snippet: is a proper coloring of the edges

annotations.note: No dedicated proper edge coloring exists (`EdgeLabeling` explicitly is not it), but it is expressible as a `Coloring` of `lineGraph`.

annotations.label: k-edge-coloring

anchor.snippet: is called a k -edge-coloring

annotations.note: Expressible as `(lineGraph G).Colorable k` but there is no dedicated edge-coloring API.

annotations.label: Chromatic index

anchor.snippet: is the chromatic index , or edge chromatic number

annotations.note: Could be defined as `(lineGraph G).chromaticNumber`, but no chromatic index is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tait coloring

anchor.snippet: A Tait coloring is a 3-edge coloring of a cubic graph

annotations.note: No edge coloring nor Tait coloring is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Four color theorem via Tait coloring

anchor.snippet: every planar cubic bridgeless graph admits a Tait coloring

annotations.note: No planar graphs, edge colorings, or the four color theorem are in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Total coloring

anchor.snippet: Total coloring is a type of coloring on the vertices and edges of a graph

annotations.note: Combined vertex-and-edge total colorings are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Total chromatic number

anchor.snippet: is the fewest colors needed in any total coloring of G

annotations.note: Requires total colorings, which are absent.

annotations.label: Face coloring

anchor.snippet: the face coloring is the dual of the vertex coloring problem

annotations.note: Face coloring requires planar embeddings/duals, which are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tutte's flow theorem

anchor.snippet: then G admits a nowhere-zero k -flow

annotations.note: Mathlib's `SimpleGraph.tutte` is the perfect-matching theorem; nowhere-zero flows are not formalized.

annotations.label: Unlabeled coloring

anchor.snippet: is an orbit of a coloring under the action of the automorphism group of the graph

annotations.note: Orbits of colorings under the automorphism group are not formalized.

annotations.label: Unlabeled chromatic polynomial analogue

anchor.snippet: which counts the number of unlabeled colorings of a graph from a given finite color set

annotations.note: No counting function for unlabeled colorings exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trivial upper bound χ ≤ n

anchor.section: Upper bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: Assigning distinct colors to distinct vertices always yields a proper coloring

annotations.note: `chromaticNumber_le_card` gives `G.chromaticNumber ≤ Fintype.card V` via the tautological self-coloring.

annotations.label: 1-colorable graphs are edgeless

anchor.section: Upper bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: The only graphs that can be 1-colored are edgeless graphs

annotations.note: `chromaticNumber_eq_one_iff` shows χ = 1 ↔ `G = ⊥` (edgeless) and nonempty, capturing 1-colorability of edgeless graphs.

annotations.label: Complete graph chromatic number

anchor.section: Upper bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: of n vertices requires

annotations.note: `chromaticNumber_top` proves the complete graph (`⊤`) on V has chromatic number `Fintype.card V`.

annotations.label: Lower bound from edges

anchor.section: Upper bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: In an optimal coloring there must be at least one of the graph's m edges between every pair of color classes

annotations.note: The edge-count lower bound on χ is not formalized.

annotations.label: χ-bounded family

anchor.section: Upper bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: of graphs is χ -bounded if there is some function

annotations.note: The notion of a χ-bounded family of graphs is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Perfect graphs χ-bounded by clique number

anchor.section: Upper bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: For the family of the perfect graphs this function is

annotations.note: Perfect graphs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 2-colorable graphs are bipartite

anchor.section: Upper bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: The 2-colorable graphs are exactly the bipartite graphs

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `IsBipartite := Colorable 2` and proves it equivalent to having a bipartition via `isBipartite_iff_exists_isBipartiteWith`.

annotations.label: Planar graphs are 4-colorable

anchor.section: Upper bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: every planar graph can be 4-colored

annotations.note: The four color theorem and planar graphs are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Greedy bound χ ≤ Δ+1

anchor.section: Upper bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: every graph can be colored with one more color than the maximum vertex degree

annotations.note: `maxDegree` exists but there is no lemma bounding chromatic number by `maxDegree + 1`.

annotations.label: Δ+1 bound tight for complete graphs and odd cycles

anchor.section: Upper bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: so for these graphs this bound is best possible

annotations.note: Odd-cycle (χ=3) and complete-graph (χ=n) chromatic numbers are computed, but the Δ+1 tightness framing is absent since the Δ+1 bound itself is unformalized.

annotations.label: Brooks' theorem

anchor.section: Upper bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: for a connected, simple graph G , unless G is a complete graph or an odd cycle

annotations.note: Brooks' theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Clique number lower bound

anchor.section: Lower bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: then at least k colors are needed to color that clique

annotations.note: `IsClique.card_le_chromaticNumber` (and `cliqueNum_le_chromaticNumber`) give ω(G) ≤ χ(G).

annotations.label: Clique bound tight for perfect graphs

anchor.section: Lower bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: For perfect graphs this bound is tight

annotations.note: Perfect graphs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hoffman's bound

anchor.section: Lower bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: be a real symmetric matrix such that

annotations.note: Hoffman's spectral lower bound on χ is not formalized.

annotations.label: Vector chromatic number bound

anchor.section: Lower bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: be a positive semi-definite matrix such that

annotations.note: The vector chromatic number is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lovász number lower bound

anchor.section: Lower bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: The Lovász number of a complementary graph is also a lower bound on the chromatic number

annotations.note: The Lovász number is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fractional chromatic number lower bound

anchor.section: Lower bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: The fractional chromatic number of a graph is a lower bound on the chromatic number as well

annotations.note: The fractional chromatic number is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ordering of the lower bounds

anchor.section: Lower bounds on the chromatic number
anchor.snippet: These bounds are ordered as follows

annotations.note: These spectral/fractional/Lovász bounds and their ordering are not formalized.

annotations.label: Large cliques imply high χ, not conversely

anchor.snippet: but the opposite is not true

annotations.note: The forward direction ω ≤ χ is `cliqueNum_le_chromaticNumber`, but the non-converse (triangle-free high-χ graphs) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Grötzsch graph

anchor.snippet: The Grötzsch graph is an example of a 4-chromatic graph without a triangle

annotations.note: The Grötzsch graph is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Triangle-free graphs of arbitrarily high χ

anchor.snippet: There exist triangle-free graphs with arbitrarily high chromatic number

annotations.note: The Mycielski construction / this existence result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Burling graphs

anchor.snippet: whose intersection graph is triangle-free and requires arbitrarily many colors to be properly colored

annotations.note: Burling graphs and box intersection graphs are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Boxes and segments not χ-bounded

anchor.snippet: are not χ -bounded

annotations.note: Geometric intersection graphs and χ-boundedness are not formalized.

annotations.label: High χ requires high maximum degree

anchor.snippet: graphs with high chromatic number must have high maximum degree

annotations.note: No lemma relating chromatic number to maximum degree exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Erdős: high girth and high χ

anchor.snippet: There exist graphs of arbitrarily high girth and chromatic number

annotations.note: `girth` is defined but Erdős's high-girth high-χ existence theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Edge coloring = vertex coloring of line graph

anchor.snippet: An edge coloring of G is a vertex coloring of its line graph

annotations.note: `lineGraph` and `Coloring` both exist so the identity is expressible, but it is not stated as a lemma.

annotations.label: Chromatic index at least max degree

anchor.snippet: Since all edges incident to the same vertex need their own color

annotations.note: No edge coloring/chromatic index is defined, so this bound is absent.

annotations.label: Kőnig's theorem

anchor.snippet: if G is bipartite

annotations.note: König's edge-coloring theorem (χ' = Δ for bipartite graphs) is not formalized as edge coloring is absent.

annotations.label: Vizing's theorem

anchor.snippet: has edge-chromatic number

annotations.note: Vizing's theorem is not in Mathlib (no edge coloring).

annotations.label: Gallai–Hasse–Roy–Vitaver theorem

anchor.snippet: if and only if it has an acyclic orientation for which the longest path has length at most k

annotations.note: Acyclic orientations and this theorem relating χ to longest paths are not formalized.

annotations.label: Planar colorings dual to nowhere-zero flows

anchor.snippet: vertex colorings are essentially dual to nowhere-zero flows

annotations.note: Nowhere-zero flows and planar duality are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: de Bruijn–Erdős theorem

anchor.snippet: If all finite subgraphs of an infinite graph G are k -colorable, then so is G

annotations.note: This compactness theorem (homs to a finite graph) specializes to the complete graph to give exactly the de Bruijn–Erdős coloring theorem.

annotations.label: Fawcett's infinite full coloring theorem

anchor.snippet: it admits an infinite full coloring

annotations.note: Full/complete colorings and this theorem are not formalized.

annotations.label: Reed's conjecture

anchor.snippet: is that the value is essentially closer to the lower bound

annotations.note: Reed's conjecture is not formalized.

annotations.label: Chromatic number of the plane

anchor.snippet: is unknown, although it is one of 5, 6, or 7

annotations.note: The Hadwiger–Nelson problem is not formalized (the unit-distance graph file does not address it).

annotations.label: Hadwiger, Erdős–Faber–Lovász, Albertson conjectures

anchor.snippet: every graph with chromatic number k has a complete graph on k vertices as a minor

annotations.note: Graph minors and these conjectures are not formalized.

annotations.label: Birkhoff–Lewis conjecture

anchor.snippet: they conjectured that for planar graphs

annotations.note: Requires chromatic polynomials and planar graphs, both absent.

annotations.label: Chromatic polynomial characterization problems

anchor.snippet: to characterize graphs which have the same chromatic polynomial

annotations.note: Chromatic polynomials are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 2-coloring = bipartiteness in linear time

anchor.snippet: is equivalent to determining whether or not the graph is bipartite

annotations.note: The mathematical equivalence (2-colorable ↔ bipartite) is `chromaticNumber_le_two_iff_isBipartite`, but the linear-time algorithmic claim is not formalized.

annotations.label: Perfect graphs colorable in polynomial time

anchor.snippet: can be computed in polynomial time using semidefinite programming

annotations.note: Perfect graphs and complexity-theoretic claims are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed-form chromatic polynomials

anchor.snippet: Closed formulas for chromatic polynomials are known for many classes of graphs

annotations.note: Chromatic polynomials are not formalized.

annotations.label: Low branch-width polynomial time

anchor.snippet: then it can be solved in polynomial time using dynamic programming

annotations.note: Branch-width and algorithmic complexity are not formalized.

annotations.label: Brute-force coloring search

anchor.snippet: Brute-force search for a k -coloring considers each of the

annotations.note: Coloring algorithms and their complexity are not formalized.

annotations.label: Dynamic programming colorability bound

anchor.snippet: Using dynamic programming and a bound on the number of maximal independent sets

annotations.note: These algorithmic runtime bounds are not formalized.

annotations.label: Inclusion–exclusion colorability bound

anchor.snippet: Using the principle of inclusion–exclusion and Yates 's algorithm for the fast zeta transform

annotations.note: This coloring algorithm runtime result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Faster 3- and 4-colorability algorithms

anchor.snippet: Faster algorithms are known for 3- and 4-colorability

annotations.note: Specialized colorability algorithms are not formalized.

annotations.label: Faster 5- and 6-colorability algorithms

anchor.snippet: Exponentially faster algorithms are also known for 5- and 6-colorability

annotations.note: Specialized colorability algorithms are not formalized.

annotations.label: Contraction of a graph

anchor.snippet: is the graph obtained by identifying the vertices u and v

annotations.note: Vertex contraction of simple graphs is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chromatic number recurrence (Zykov)

anchor.snippet: The chromatic number satisfies the recurrence relation

annotations.note: The Zykov deletion-contraction recurrence is not formalized (no contraction).

annotations.label: Chromatic polynomial recurrence

anchor.snippet: The chromatic polynomial satisfies the following recurrence relation

annotations.note: Chromatic polynomials and their recurrence are not formalized.

annotations.label: Deletion–contraction algorithm

anchor.snippet: a recursive procedure called the deletion–contraction algorithm

annotations.note: The deletion-contraction algorithm is not formalized.

annotations.label: Deletion–contraction running time

anchor.snippet: The running time satisfies the same recurrence relation as the Fibonacci numbers

annotations.note: This running-time analysis is not formalized.

annotations.label: Greedy coloring algorithm

anchor.snippet: The greedy algorithm considers the vertices in a specific order

annotations.note: The greedy coloring algorithm is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Optimal greedy ordering exists

anchor.snippet: There exists an ordering that leads to a greedy coloring with the optimal number of

annotations.note: No greedy coloring results are formalized.

annotations.label: Crown graph greedy worst case

anchor.snippet: the crown graph on n vertices can be 2-colored

annotations.note: Crown graphs and greedy worst cases are not formalized.

annotations.label: Greedy optimal on chordal graphs

anchor.snippet: the greedy coloring algorithm can be used to find optimal colorings in polynomial time

annotations.note: Chordal graphs and greedy optimality are not formalized.

annotations.label: Welsh–Powell degree ordering bound

anchor.snippet: the resulting greedy coloring uses at most

annotations.note: The Welsh–Powell bound is not formalized.

annotations.label: Grundy number

anchor.snippet: is called the Grundy number of a graph

annotations.note: The Grundy number is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: DSatur and RLF heuristics

anchor.snippet: are the DSatur and recursive largest first (RLF) algorithms

annotations.note: Coloring heuristics are not formalized.

annotations.label: Degree of saturation

anchor.snippet: This is defined as the degree of saturation of a given vertex

annotations.note: Saturation degree is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Recursive largest first algorithm

anchor.snippet: by constructing each color class one at a time

annotations.note: The RLF heuristic is not formalized.

annotations.label: DSatur worst-case complexity

anchor.snippet: The worst-case complexity of DSatur is

annotations.note: DSatur and its complexity are not formalized.

annotations.label: DSatur and RLF exact on special graphs

anchor.snippet: DSatur and RLF are exact for bipartite , cycle , and wheel graphs

annotations.note: These exactness results for heuristics are not formalized.

annotations.label: LOCAL model coloring upper bound

anchor.snippet: can be c -colored in the deterministic LOCAL model

annotations.note: Distributed LOCAL-model algorithms are not formalized.

annotations.label: LOCAL model matching lower bound

anchor.snippet: A matching lower bound of

annotations.note: Distributed model lower bounds are not formalized.

annotations.label: No deterministic coloring of symmetric graphs

anchor.snippet: a deterministic distributed algorithm cannot find a proper vertex coloring

annotations.note: Distributed computation impossibility results are not formalized.

annotations.label: Distributed greedy needs Θ(n) rounds

anchor.snippet: requires Θ( n ) communication rounds in the worst case

annotations.note: Distributed round-complexity results are not formalized.

annotations.label: Cole–Vishkin one-step color reduction

anchor.snippet: there is a distributed algorithm that reduces the number of colors from n to O (log n ) in one synchronous communication step

annotations.note: The Cole–Vishkin algorithm is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cole–Vishkin 3-coloring of cycle

anchor.snippet: it is possible to obtain a 3-coloring of an n -cycle in O ( log * n ) communication steps

annotations.note: This distributed cycle-coloring result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Linial's lower bound

anchor.snippet: any deterministic distributed algorithm requires Ω( log * n ) communication steps to reduce an n -coloring to a 3-coloring in an n -cycle

annotations.note: Linial's distributed lower bound is not formalized.

annotations.label: Panconesi–Rizzi distributed edge coloring

anchor.snippet: achieve a (2Δ − 1)-coloring in O (Δ + log * n ) time in this model

annotations.note: Distributed edge-coloring algorithms are not formalized.

annotations.label: Decentralized coloring exists

anchor.snippet: efficient decentralized algorithms exist that will color a graph if a proper coloring exists

annotations.note: Decentralized coloring algorithms are not formalized.

annotations.label: Learning automata coloring almost surely

anchor.snippet: to find a proper graph coloring with probability one

annotations.note: Learning-automata coloring results are not formalized.

annotations.label: NP-completeness of k-coloring

anchor.snippet: except for the cases k ∈ {0,1,2}

annotations.note: Complexity classes like NP-completeness of coloring are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: NP-hardness of chromatic number

anchor.snippet: it is NP-hard to compute the chromatic number

annotations.note: NP-hardness of the chromatic number is not formalized.

annotations.label: 3-coloring NP-complete on 4-regular planar graphs

anchor.snippet: The 3-coloring problem remains NP-complete even on 4-regular planar graphs

annotations.note: This NP-completeness result is not formalized.

annotations.label: 3-coloring linear time for max degree 3

anchor.snippet: the 3-coloring problem can be solved in linear time

annotations.note: This algorithmic result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lexicographically smallest 4-coloring NP-complete

anchor.snippet: finding the lexicographically smallest 4-coloring of a planar graph is NP-complete

annotations.note: This NP-completeness result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Best known approximation factor

anchor.snippet: The best known approximation algorithm computes a coloring of size at most within a factor

annotations.note: Approximation algorithms for coloring are not formalized.

annotations.label: Hardness of approximating chromatic number

anchor.snippet: approximating the chromatic number within n 1− ε is NP-hard

annotations.note: Hardness of approximation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hardness of coloring k-colorable graphs

anchor.snippet: It is also NP-hard to color a 3-colorable graph with 5 colors

annotations.note: This hardness result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Chromatic polynomial coefficients #P-hard

anchor.snippet: Computing the coefficients of the chromatic polynomial is #P-hard

annotations.note: Chromatic polynomials and #P-hardness are not formalized.

annotations.label: Evaluating chromatic polynomial #P-hard

anchor.snippet: is #P-hard at any rational point k except for k = 1 and k = 2

annotations.note: Chromatic polynomial evaluation hardness is not formalized.

annotations.label: No FPRAS for chromatic polynomial

anchor.snippet: There is no FPRAS for evaluating the chromatic polynomial at any rational point k ≥ 1.5

annotations.note: FPRAS results for chromatic polynomials are not formalized.

annotations.label: Vizing's proof gives Δ+1 edge coloring

anchor.snippet: the proof of Vizing's result gives an algorithm that uses at most Δ+1 colors

annotations.note: Vizing's theorem and edge coloring are not formalized.

annotations.label: Edge chromatic number NP-complete

anchor.snippet: deciding between the two candidate values for the edge chromatic number is NP-complete

annotations.note: Edge chromatic number and its complexity are not formalized.

annotations.label: Edge chromatic number 4/3-approximation

anchor.snippet: the edge chromatic number can be approximated to within 4/3

annotations.note: Edge coloring approximation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Chromatic number equals minimum makespan

anchor.snippet: The chromatic number of the graph is exactly the minimum makespan

annotations.note: Scheduling/makespan models are not formalized.

annotations.label: Aircraft scheduling interval graph

anchor.snippet: the resulting conflict graph is an interval graph

annotations.note: Interval graphs and this application are not formalized.

annotations.label: Bandwidth allocation unit disk graph

anchor.snippet: the resulting conflict graph is a unit disk graph

annotations.note: Unit disk graphs are not defined in Mathlib (the unit-distance graph file is unrelated).

annotations.label: Interference graph

anchor.snippet: The compiler constructs an interference graph

annotations.note: Interference graphs for register allocation are not formalized.

annotations.label: k-coloring gives register assignment

anchor.snippet: any set of variables needed at the same time can be stored in at most k registers

annotations.note: This register-allocation application is not formalized.

annotations.label: Theorem on friends and strangers

anchor.snippet: there will be a monochromatic triangle

annotations.note: Edge-2-coloring infrastructure (`EdgeLabeling`/`TopEdgeLabeling`) exists toward Ramsey theory, but the monochromatic-triangle (R(3,3)≤6) theorem is not proved.

annotations.label: Modular coloring

anchor.snippet: Modular coloring is a type of graph coloring in which the color of each vertex is the sum of the colors of its adjacent vertices

annotations.note: Modular coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Color sum

anchor.snippet: is the sum of all of the adjacent vertices to

annotations.note: The modular color sum is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Modular k-coloring

anchor.snippet: for every pair of adjacent vertices

annotations.note: Modular k-colorings are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Modular chromatic number

anchor.snippet: is the minimum value of

annotations.note: The modular chromatic number is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Modular coloring worked example

anchor.snippet: If none of the adjacent vertices have equal color sums

annotations.note: This modular-coloring example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Adjacent-vertex-distinguishing total coloring

anchor.snippet: A total coloring with the additional restriction that any two adjacent vertices have different color sets

annotations.note: Total colorings and this variant are not formalized.

annotations.label: Acyclic coloring

anchor.snippet: Every 2-chromatic subgraph is acyclic

annotations.note: Acyclic coloring is not defined in Mathlib (only `IsAcyclic` for graphs).

annotations.label: b-coloring

anchor.snippet: Each color class contains a vertex that has a neighbor in all other color classes.

annotations.note: b-coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bounded coloring

anchor.snippet: A coloring in which the number of vertices per color is bounded

annotations.note: Bounded coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Centered coloring

anchor.snippet: Every connected induced subgraph has a color that is used exactly once

annotations.note: Centered coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Circular coloring

anchor.snippet: Motivated by task systems in which production proceeds in a cyclic way

annotations.note: Circular coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cocoloring

anchor.snippet: An improper vertex coloring where every color class induces an independent set or a clique

annotations.note: Cocoloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complete coloring

anchor.snippet: Every pair of colors appears on at least one edge

annotations.note: Complete coloring (achromatic) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Defective coloring

anchor.snippet: An improper vertex coloring where every color class induces a bounded degree subgraph.

annotations.note: Defective coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Distinguishing coloring

anchor.snippet: An improper vertex coloring that destroys all the symmetries of the graph

annotations.note: Distinguishing coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equitable coloring

anchor.snippet: The sizes of color classes differ by at most one

annotations.note: Equitable coloring is not defined (Mathlib's `IsEquipartition` is for Szemerédi regularity, not coloring).

annotations.label: Exact coloring

anchor.snippet: Every pair of colors appears on exactly one edge

annotations.note: Exact coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fractional coloring

anchor.snippet: Vertices may have multiple colors, and on each edge the sum of the color parts of each vertex is not greater than one

annotations.note: Fractional coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Detour-distance coloring

anchor.snippet: Uses the length of the longest path between two vertices, also known as the detour distance

annotations.note: Detour-distance coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Harmonious coloring

anchor.snippet: Every pair of colors appears on at most one edge

annotations.note: Harmonious coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Incidence coloring

anchor.snippet: Each adjacent incidence of vertex and edge is colored with distinct colors

annotations.note: Incidence coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kaleidoscopic coloring

anchor.snippet: A set of vertex colorings induced by perfect matchings of edge-colored Graphs .

annotations.note: Kaleidoscopic coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Interval (ordered) coloring

anchor.snippet: A partition of vertices of an ordered graph into intervals such that two adjacent vertices in the same interval

annotations.note: Interval (ordered) coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Interval edge coloring

anchor.snippet: A color of edges meeting in a common vertex must be contiguous (unrelated to the above entry)

annotations.note: Interval edge coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: List coloring

anchor.snippet: Each vertex chooses from a list of colors

annotations.note: List coloring (choosability) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: List edge coloring

anchor.snippet: Each edge chooses from a list of colors

annotations.note: List edge coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: L(h,k)-coloring

anchor.snippet: Difference of colors at adjacent vertices is at least h and difference of colors of vertices at a distance two is at least k .

annotations.note: L(h,k)-coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Oriented coloring

anchor.snippet: Takes into account orientation of edges of the graph

annotations.note: Oriented coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Packing coloring

anchor.snippet: The distance between any two vertices with the same color must be greater than the assigned color's value

annotations.note: Packing coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Path coloring

anchor.snippet: Models a routing problem in graphs

annotations.note: Path coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Radio coloring

anchor.snippet: Sum of the distance between the vertices and the difference of their colors is greater than k + 1, where k is a positive integer.

annotations.note: Radio coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ranking (ordered) coloring

anchor.snippet: If two vertices have the same color i , then every path between them contain a vertex with color greater than i

annotations.note: Ranking (ordered) coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Subcoloring

anchor.snippet: An improper vertex coloring where every color class induces a union of cliques

annotations.note: Subcoloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sum coloring

anchor.snippet: The criterion of minimalization is the sum of colors

annotations.note: Sum coloring (chromatic sum) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Star coloring

anchor.snippet: Every 2-chromatic subgraph is a disjoint collection of stars

annotations.note: Star coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Strong coloring

anchor.snippet: Every color appears in every partition of equal size exactly once

annotations.note: Strong coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Strong edge coloring

anchor.snippet: Edges are colored such that each color class induces a matching (equivalent to coloring the square of the line graph)

annotations.note: Strong edge coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: T-coloring

anchor.snippet: Absolute value of the difference between two colors of adjacent vertices must not belong to fixed set T

annotations.note: T-coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Total coloring (list entry)

anchor.snippet: Vertices and edges are colored

annotations.note: Total coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Triangle-free edge coloring

anchor.snippet: The edges are colored so that each color class forms a triangle-free subgraph

annotations.note: Triangle-free edge coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weak coloring

anchor.snippet: An improper vertex coloring where every non-isolated node has at least one neighbor with a different color

annotations.note: Weak coloring is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coloring of signed and gain graphs

anchor.snippet: Coloring can also be considered for signed graphs and gain graphs .

annotations.note: Signed/gain graphs and their colorings are not defined in Mathlib.
